expectations.text: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L37 header '| Concern | Owner | Notes |' introduces a 26-row ownership table (L38-64).

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L113-131: '## Conflict resolution' with markdown table header '| Boundary | Agent A | Agent B | Resolution |' (L117) and 12 boundary rows (e.g. L119 'In-product microcopy ... | `marketing-writer` | `frontend-designer` | `frontend-designer` wins...'). Previous run's numbered-list failure is fixed.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a named Sizing Rationale section defending the chosen number of agents (explicitly evaluating whether 6 is right)
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L18 '## Sizing rationale'; L20 defends six explicitly: 'Six agents is the right number... Four would have been under-specialised; eight would have introduced overlap... Six covers every concern...'.

expectations.text: Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory) — path substitution OK for sandbox-blocked .claude/
expectations.evidence: outputs/agents/ contains six lowercase .md files (product-lead.md, frontend-designer.md, marketing-writer.md, dx-engineer.md, local-seo-specialist.md, supabase-architect.md); each frontmatter has name/description/tools/model/memory (e.g. product-lead.md L1-7).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
expectations.evidence: All six agent files declare 'memory: project' at frontmatter L6 (product-lead.md L6, frontend-designer.md L6, marketing-writer.md L6, dx-engineer.md L6, local-seo-specialist.md L6, supabase-architect.md L6).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger with inclusion and exclusion criteria
expectations.evidence: Every description uses 'Use when... Also triggers on/when... Do NOT use for... (hand to X)' structure (e.g. product-lead.md L3, supabase-architect.md L3, local-seo-specialist.md L3).

expectations.text: Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
expectations.evidence: All six files contain the six headings in order (e.g. product-lead.md L9/13/21/29/42/50; supabase-architect.md L9/13/21/29/42/50; same structure across the remaining four).

expectations.text: Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
expectations.evidence: All six files include bold '**Owns:**', '**Does not own:**', '**Hands off to:**' subheadings (e.g. product-lead.md L31/33/35, supabase-architect.md L31/33/35, dx-engineer.md L31/33/35).

expectations.text: No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L66 states 'No concern is owned by two agents'; the 26 rows at L38-64 each have a single Owner. The schema.org edge is split into two distinct concerns ('Schema.org markup specification' at L52 vs 'Schema.org on-page implementation' at L53) so no row shares ownership.

expectations.text: No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox
expectations.evidence: ls of outputs/ shows only .claude/, agent-calibration/, agents/, activation-summary.md, COMPANY.md — no agent-company/ directory.

expectations.text: Calibration transcripts, if persisted, live under .claude/agent-calibration/ or agent-calibration/ (never elsewhere)
expectations.evidence: outputs/agent-calibration/ and outputs/.claude/agent-calibration/ exist (both empty); no transcripts appear anywhere else. Vacuously satisfied.

expectations.text: At least one agent references actual codebase paths like src/app/, tailwind.config.ts, or supabase/ in its body
expectations.evidence: frontend-designer.md L11 'Next.js App Router (`src/app/`), Tailwind' and L27 '`tailwind.config.ts`'; supabase-architect.md L31 '`supabase/migrations/`' and L31 '`supabase/functions/`'; dx-engineer.md L56 '`src/app/`, Tailwind theming in `tailwind.config.ts`, Supabase config under `supabase/`'.

expectations.text: An activation summary artefact exists reporting what was built, calibration results per agent, and routing sanity check result
expectations.evidence: activation-summary.md has 'Files written' (L3-14), per-agent '## Calibration results' 6-probe tables for all six agents (L22-87), and '## Routing sanity check' (L90-107).

expectations.text: The routing sanity check was run and its result is reported in the activation summary
expectations.evidence: activation-summary.md L90-107 presents a 10-prompt routing table with routed-to agent and notes per row; L107 reports 'Run twice in simulation — same routing both runs. No ambiguous, no coverage gaps, no orphans.'

expectations.text: Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content
expectations.evidence: Each agent's Voice has two blockquoted examples with concrete numbers/content. product-lead.md L17-19 ('£29 for one feature... Saturday rush' / '11pm after service'); frontend-designer.md L17-19 ('44px tap targets' / 'tailwind.config.ts 50-950 scale'); marketing-writer.md L17-19; dx-engineer.md L17-19 ('22 seconds on a M2' / 'Vitest + Playwright'); local-seo-specialist.md L17-19 ('proximity, prominence, and relevance' / 'Rich Results Test'); supabase-architect.md L17-19 ('RLS policy... Write the test first' / 'CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY... Friday menu refresh').

expectations.text: Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
expectations.evidence: Each agent has three named frameworks with concrete criteria. product-lead.md L23-27 (JTBD clustering; North-star activation funnel with specific steps; Willingness-to-pay probe '3 interviews'). frontend-designer.md L23-27 (WCAG 2.2 AA 2.5.8/1.4.3/2.4.11; Core Web Vitals LCP 2.5s CLS 0.1 INP 200ms; Token discipline). marketing-writer.md L23-27 (PAS with cost line; Jargon kill-list; Proof over promise). dx-engineer.md L23-27 (Inner-loop budget HMR<300ms, unit<30s, tc<15s; Risk-weighted test pyramid; One-command onboarding <10min). local-seo-specialist.md L23-27 (Proximity/Prominence/Relevance; Review-response SLA 4h/24h/72h; Schema.org conformance). supabase-architect.md L23-27 (RLS-first; Migration safety checklist; Data-residency map).

expectations.text: At least one framework per agent names a domain-specific source (regulator, named methodology, official standard, published benchmark) — not a generic business tool
expectations.evidence: product-lead.md L23: JTBD (named methodology). frontend-designer.md L23-25: WCAG 2.2 AA with success criteria 2.5.8/1.4.3/2.4.11 + Core Web Vitals (Google official). marketing-writer.md L23: PAS (named copy methodology). dx-engineer.md L25: Risk-weighted test pyramid with named tools Vitest + Playwright. local-seo-specialist.md L23-27: Google's Proximity/Prominence/Relevance triangle + Schema.org + Google Rich Results Test. supabase-architect.md L27: GDPR Article 5 and 17.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
expectations.evidence: Each file has 5 role-specific bulleted failure modes. product-lead.md L44-48 (horizontal-SaaS roadmap copy, pricing without WTP, training-data pricing, MVP over-scope, enthusiasm≠WTP). frontend-designer.md L44-48 (raw hex, desktop-first mobile mistake, hover-only affordances, memory-quoted shadcn/Tailwind APIs, skipped Lighthouse). marketing-writer.md L44-48. dx-engineer.md L43-47. local-seo-specialist.md L44-48 (ranking promises, platform-policy from memory, skipped Rich Results Test, policy-violating tactics, vanity metrics). supabase-architect.md L44-48 (RLS-disabled tables, blocking ALTER on hot tables, Supabase-limits from memory, anon-key complacency, realtime leaks).

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
expectations.evidence: product-lead.md L46 'Quoting current restaurant-tech market statistics... from memory'; frontend-designer.md L47 'Quoting current shadcn/ui, Tailwind v4, or Next.js App Router API shapes from memory'; marketing-writer.md L46 'Quoting current platform policy language... from memory'; dx-engineer.md L45 'Quoting current Next.js, Supabase CLI, or Vercel config flags from memory'; local-seo-specialist.md L45 'Quoting current Google Business Profile policy... from memory'; supabase-architect.md L46 'Quoting current Supabase platform limits... from memory'.

expectations.text: The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
expectations.evidence: Spot-checks align. supabase-architect: COMPANY.md L54-58 (Postgres schema, RLS, Auth/Realtime/Edge, residency, GDPR facts) matches supabase-architect.md L31. frontend-designer: COMPANY.md L42-45, L48, L53 matches frontend-designer.md L31. marketing-writer: COMPANY.md L46-47 matches marketing-writer.md L31. product-lead: COMPANY.md L39-41 matches product-lead.md L31. local-seo-specialist: COMPANY.md L49-52 matches local-seo-specialist.md L31. dx-engineer: COMPANY.md L59-64 matches dx-engineer.md L31. Schema.org split (spec vs implementation) mirrored correctly on both sides.

expectations.text: Tools field is scoped per agent (no padding)
expectations.evidence: Differentiated: product-lead/frontend-designer/marketing-writer/local-seo-specialist get Read,Write,Edit,Grep,Glob,WebSearch,WebFetch (no Bash — pure strategy/content/research roles). dx-engineer (L4) and supabase-architect (L4) add Bash for CI/migrations. local-seo-specialist (L4) leads with Read,WebSearch,WebFetch reflecting research-primary use. Each toolset maps to the role's actual work.

claims: Patch confirmed: Conflict Resolution is now a markdown table with the required Boundary | Agent A | Agent B | Resolution columns (COMPANY.md L117) — the single failure from the prior run is resolved.

12 boundary rows in the conflict table cover every genuinely contested edge named in the handoff rules (microcopy, pricing page, GDPR copy, schema.org, review triage, Tailwind tokens, Storybook, data-model/UI, scope-via-schema, review-platform integration, preview/prod parity, dev vs marketing docs).
Calibration was honestly disclosed as content-level simulation due to Agent-tool unavailability in the sandbox (activation-summary.md L17-20).
Every agent anchors to the stated stack — src/app/, tailwind.config.ts, supabase/ — rather than a generic Next.js/Supabase caricature.

eval_feedback.overall: All 24 assertions pass on this re-run (prior run: 23/24). The skill patch requiring a markdown table with columns Boundary | Agent A | Agent B | Resolution was followed literally and the content is richer than the previous numbered-list version, not poorer — each row resolves a real edge with a named tie-breaker.
observations: Assertion 11 remains vacuously satisfied (no calibration transcripts were persisted). If calibration-artefact persistence matters, tighten to 'at least one calibration artefact MUST be persisted under one of these paths'.

Assertion 22's wording 'references COMPANY.md ... for conflict resolution' is satisfied by phrasing like 'boundary edge'; agent files do not all use the literal phrase 'conflict resolution'. A stricter reviewer could challenge this — consider requiring the literal phrase or accept the current boundary-edge phrasing explicitly.
